questions.text:
______ are precursors of enzymes.
a. Zymogens
b. Ribosomes
c. RNA
d. Protein
Which of the option given above is/are correct?

Which of the following statement/s is/are correct?
a. Enzymes are organic catalyst that accelerate biochemical reaction.
b. Enzymes are made up from proteins.
c. Enzyme activity is not depends upon temperature.
d. Amylase is an enzyme that act on starch.
Which of the following statement is not included in Article 164?
options: The Chief Minister shall be appointed by the Governor
The Council of Ministers shall not be collectively responsible to the State Legislature
The other Minister shall be appointed by the Governor on the advice of the Chief Minister
A Minister who for any period of six consecutive months is not a member of the Legislature of the State shall at the expiration of that period cease to be a minister

questions.text:
Indian citizenship of a person can be terminated if
A. A person voluntary acquire the citizenship of some other country.
B. A person who has become a citizen through registration is sentenced to imprisonment for 18 months within five years of his registration.
C. The government of India is satisfied that citizenship was obtained by fraud.
D. A person has shown disloyalty to the Constitution of India.

Which of the following is/are correct pair/s with regard to acceptance in the Indian Constitution?
A. America â€“ Post of Vice President
B. Canada â€“ The concept of 'Union' for the Central Government
C. South Africa â€“ Election method of Rajya Sabha
D. Ireland â€“ Nomination method of the members of the Rajya Sabha by the President who have done special work in specific area

Which of the following is/are the main objective/s of Committee of Parliament on Official language?
A. To review the progress made in the use of Hindi for official purposes of the Union and to make recommendations to increase the use of Hindi.
B. Looking into the linguistic demands for new States and make suggestions in that regard.
C. Identify the endangered language and make recommendations to protect and preserve them.
D. Examine the vernacular education and make recommendations for effective implementation of the three languages formula.

Consider the following statement/s with reference to the President of Zilla Parishad. [CANCELLED]
A. If 1/5th of the total number of members of the Zilla Parishad moves a motion of no confidence and passes it, the President has to resign from office.
B. Sudhakarrao Naik's cabinet took the decision to give ministerial status to the President of Zilla Parishad.
C. Vasantrao Naik's cabinet took the decision to give ministerial status to the President of Zilla Parishad.
D. Vasantdada Patil's cabinet took the decision to give ministerial status to the President of Zilla Parishad.
Select the incorrect statements from the above.
questions.explanation: This question was cancelled by the examination authority.

II. State Finance Commission

options: Only III and IV are correct
Only I, III and IV are correct
Only I and IV are correct

questions.text:
Key Provisions of Lokpal and Lokayukta (Amendment) Act, 2016:
A. It amended the Lokpal and Lokayukta Act, 2013 (which came into force on 16 January 2014).
B. It also amended Section 44 of the 2013 Act that deals with the Provision of furnishing details of assets and liabilities of Public Servants.
C. Made it compulsory that all States should set up the office of the Lokayukta within one year.
D. The Amendment has removed the period of 30 days for furnishing details of assets and liabilities of Public Servants.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
Which of the following is/are a duties of National Commission for Backward Classes?
options: To enquire into specific complaints with respect to the deprivation of rights and safeguards of the socially and educationally backward classes
To participate and advise on the socio-economic development of the socially and educationally backward classes
To discharge other functions in relation to the protection, welfare, development and advancement of socially and educationally backward classes

questions.text:

National Party of India â€“ Year of establishment

questions.text:
Which of the following are the objectives of the 'SVAMITVA' Scheme?
A. Ensure financial stability to villagers by letting them use their property to take loans.
B. Create precise land records for urban planning and implementation.
C. Lessen property related disputes.
D. Determine property tax.

Consider the following statements regarding 'Zero Hour' of Lok Sabha:
A. The time immediately following the question hour and laying of papers and before any listed business is taken up in the house has come to be known as the zero hour.
B. For raising matters during the zero hour members has to give notice between 8.00 a.m. to 9.30 a.m. everyday to the Speaker.
C. At present, thirty matters per day as per their priority are allowed to be raised during zero hour.
D. There is no maximum limit on the number of matters that can be raised on any given day as there is no provision in rules about Zero Hour.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

Which of the following statements are correct regarding the legal relationship between the Central and State Government in India?
A. The subject of transfer of property other than agricultural land is included in the Concurrent List.
B. The subject of wealth tax on property other than agricultural land is included in the Union List.
C. The subject of entertainment tax is included in the Concurrent List.
D. The subject of 'Antiquities Museum' is included in the State List.

After the extensive discussion in the Constituent Assembly, the following provisions was/were included. Which Article does not include?
I. Fundamental duties under Art. 51A
II. Constitutional Amendment under Art. 368
III. National emergency under Art. 352
IV. Ordinance under Art. 123
options: All of the above are correct
None of the above are correct

C. To eradicate malnutrition by 2030 is the objective of this Campaign.

options: A, C and D are correct
A, B and D are correct

[Passage based] There is an increased demand for fish as it is a good source of protein. About 70% of world's marine fish stocks are now overfished. Aquaculture is continuously expanded but it is necessary to manage environmental risks. For this the aquaculture sector needs to devise effective and environment friendly methods.
Select the most appropriate conclusion:

options.A: People should adopt total vegan diet for avoiding environmental problems.
options.B: Aquaculture of predator fishes should be banned till other environment friendly devise is designed for rearing them.
options.C: People should consume freshwater fish varieties to avoid pressure on oceans.
options.D: The farming of only certain kind of varieties is damaging the biodiversity of ecosystems.

questions.text: Ashok jumped higher than Mohan but not higher than Chetan. Naresh jumped higher than Krishna but not as high as that of Mohan. Then what is the correct descending order of players considering their jump?

Some persons are sitting on a bench. V is sitting next to An and S is next to G. G is not sitting with Aj. Aj is on the left end of the bench and S is in second position from right hand end. V is on the right side of An and to the right side of Aj. V and S are sitting together. If G and V exchange their positions select the statement that is not true.

options.A: Positions cannot be ascertained as the direction they face is not known.
options.D: An occupies the second position from one of the end.

(b) Vehicular traffic leads to emission of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases.
(c) Carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases are hazardous to the environment.
(d) Some vehicular traffic does not emit Carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases.
(e) Vehicular traffic is hazardous to the environment.

Select the number of dolls she did initially have.

options.D: Can not be determined from the given data

questions.text: Which space agency has released the first visible light images of the surface of venus from space?

Location of Kanyakumari is in the tropical hemisphere while Leh is located in the temperate hemisphere.

The decline in voter turnout at elections is chiefly explained by the decline in voting by younger people who have very different lifestyles. Older people are much more likely to vote. If this trend continues, probably this will lead to a point at which no government can be said to have a mandate to govern as the number of voters will be too small to indicate the wishes of the majority. Society needs, therefore, to find an alternative to conventional elections so that the wishes of the people can be heard.
Select one option that best expresses the flaw in the above argument.

options.A: Elections are used in all democratic countries as the only well established process of choosing a government.
options.B: With the help of social media, young people may be more likely to join pressure groups than to vote in an election.
options.C: There may be problems in using alternatives such as internet and postal voting due to remote residential places.
options.D: People who are young now may become more likely to vote as they get older.

The following question is based on the information given below :
I. A, B, C, D and E are five persons sitting in a line facing South â€“ while M, N, O, P and Q are five persons sitting in the second line parallel to the first line and facing North, facing each other.

III. C and N are diagonally opposite to each other.
IV. B who is just next to left of D, is opposite to Q.
V. M is at one end of the line.
VI. P who is just to the left of Q, is opposite to D.
If B shifts to the place of E, E shifts to the place of Q and Q shifts to the place of B, then who will be the second to the left of the person opposite to O ?

options.A: Only RI not RII is a possible reason
options.B: Only RII not RI is a possible reason
options.C: Either RI or RII but not both reasons
options.D: Neither RI nor RII is a possible reason

After M. Gandhiji came to India in 1915, which of the following problems did he handle successfully?
a. The indigo problem of Champaran in Bihar.
b. The land revenue problem of the farmers of Kheda.
c. The conflict between the mill workers and the owners in Ahmedabad city.
d. Creating a separate electorate for Muslims.

Which of the following activists led the March to the Mamledar Court of Tasgaon on 3rd September 1942?
a. Krishna Rama Kurhade
b. V. S. Page
c. Govind Khot
d. Dr. Y. K. Sovani

options.D: All of the above are correct

questions.text:
Identify the social reformer from the following description.
a. He started the Marathi newspaper 'Dnyan Prakash'.
b. The government honored him with the titles 'Justice of Peace and Rao Bahadur'.
c. He had a deep interest in music.

Which women reformer's work do you identify by reading the following statements?
A. She was active in the women's emancipation work of the organization 'Shardasadan' and 'Arya Mahila Samaj'.
B. The Government honored her with the title of 'Kaisar-i-Hind' for her social work in 1913.
C. She founded the 'Hindu Ladies Social Club' in 1894.
D. Her work for the emancipation of women was supported by Thakarsy and Tata families by providing financial assistance.

Which of the following statements are correct regarding the uprising of 1857 in Maharashtra?
a. Rangrao Page made relentless efforts to regain the lost kingdom of Chhatrapati Pratapsingh of Satara.
b. Soldiers of the 21st and 28th regiments mutinied in Kolhapur on 31st July 1857.
c. In Princely State of Nargunda, Bhave's adopted son was disowned and he rebelled in May 1858.
d. The Bhillas were defeated in the battle of Amba Pani on 11th April 1858.

The British filed a case of sedition against _________ for his writing regarding the Muzaffarpur bomb case.

Which of the following social works were done by Prarthana Samaj?
(a) N.M. Joshi tried to improve the condition of labourers by establishing the 'Social Service League'.
(b) Namdar Gokhale Founded the 'Servants of India Society' with the aim of producing good worker's for the service of the country.
(c) An Orphanage was opened in Nashik on behalf of this Samaj.
(d) Maharshi Vitthal Ramaji Shinde Founded the Depressed Classes Mission for the upliftment of the untouchables.

Which of the following statements are true about Rajarshi Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj?
(a) He legislated a compulsory and free education.
(b) He gave patronage to different arts and artists.
(c) He legalised widow re-marriage act.
(d) He established 'Shahupuri' market for the traders in Kolhapur.

Which of the following leaders have not served as Presidents of AITUC, a labour union?
(a) B.T. Randive
(b) Sarojini Naidu
(c) C.R. Das
(d) Jawaharlal Nehru

Match the following.
(a) Mukundarao Patil - (i) Vijayi Maratha
(b) V.R. Kothari - (ii) Jagruti
(c) Bhagavant Palekar - (iii) Jagaruk
(d) Shripatrao Shinde - (iv) Dinmitra
Who was the Chairperson of Indian National Congress session held in 1887 at Madras?

Match the correct pairs.
(a) An Act for the better Government of India - 1858 - (i) Independent constituency for Muslims
(b) Indian Council Act of - 1909 - (ii) Foundation of Cabinet System
(c) Government of India Act of - 1919 - (iii) Dyarchy system in State
(d) Government of India Act of - 1935 - (iv) Created the post Secretary of State for India
(v) Dyarchy System in Central

What was the reason the indigo farmers in Bengal succeeded in revolting against the landlords after the revolt of 1857?
(a) Entrepreneurship and co-operation
(b) Organization and discipline
(c) A split in the landlords
(d) Hindu-Muslim peasants unity

Match the pairs:
(a) Moplah rebellion - (i) Khandesh (Maharashtra)
(b) Kol uprising - (ii) Kerala
(c) Santhal uprising - (iii) Bihar
(d) Bhil uprising - (iv) Bangal
_________ was the Police Commissioner of Bombay had erected a gallows to hang those who took action against the British rule.

Consider the following statements:
(a) The range extending from Raireshwar to Shingnapur is called Shambhu-Mahadev mountain range.
(b) The river basins of Narmada and Tapi are separated by the Satpura mountain range.
(c) The Godavari and Bhima river basins are separated by the Harishchandra-Balaghat mountain range.
(d) The Satmala-Ajantha mountain range separates the Godavari and Tapi river basins.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options: All of the above Statements are true.

questions.text: Which of the following three districts from Maharashtra recording negative growth rate of population as per 2011 census?
Which district in Maharashtra have more iron ore mineral deposits as per statistics of 2014 by Directorate, Mining Department, Nagpur?

Match the following.
Column A (Coppen climate) - Column B (Climate type)
(a) Cwg - (i) Hot Desert
(b) BWhw - (ii) Cold and humid winter
(c) Dfc - (iii) Tropical monsoon
(d) Amw - (iv) Mesothermal

Consider the following statements:
(a) The Chenab river originates near the Zojila Pass.
(b) The source of Ravi river is near the Rohtang Pass.
(c) The source of Sutluj river is near Rakas lake.
Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Which of the following statement/s is/are correct regarding the rainfall of Maharashtra?
(a) In Maharashtra, the air pressure decreases from West to East at the beginning of the monsoon season.
(b) The distribution of rainfall in the state depends mainly on the distribution of natural vegetation.
(c) When the South-West monsoon winds move Eastward from the ghats, their vapour holding capacity increases.
(d) Rainfall increases from West to East in Vidarbha.

Which of the following statement/s is/are false?
(a) The Kamathi coal field is located in the Amb river valley.
(b) The coal field in Vardha valley is divided into Chandrapur and Yavatmal districts.
(c) The Umred coal field is located in the Kanhan river valley.
(d) The Apsara reactor with the assistance of Canada and the Cyrus reactor with the assistance of Britain have been built in Turbhe in Maharashtra.

The catchment area development program has which of the following objectives?
(a) Substantial increase in landuse and production.
(b) Stop land degradation by controlling runoff.
(c) Reducing investment as well as increasing production costs.

Observe the following statements.
(a) The Pomegranate technology research centre of Mahatma Phule Agricultural University is at Lakhmapur.
(b) Phule Yashoda wheat variety is developed by Mahatma Phule Agricultural University in 1998.
(c) The office of Maharashtra Agricultural University was at Mumbai.

Which of the following statements are true?
(a) Vaitarna and Tansa are the tributaries of river Ulhas.
(b) Krishna river basin is to the South of Khanapur plateau.
(c) Manyad river basin is in Godavari river basin.

Consider these statements:
(a) The Atal Innovation Mission (AIM) is an initiative of the Central government.
(b) AIM is instrumental in establishing ecosystem of innovation and entrepreneurship.

According to Economic Survey of India 2022-23, which of the following are sub-sectors included under Make in India 2.0?
(a) Steel
(b) Gym-equipment
(c) Toys
(d) Fisheries
As of March 2024, the Maharashtra state has the _________ share in startups recognised by Government of India at All India Level.
How many private commercial banks were nationalised on 19th July, 1969?

Match the following.
(a) Banking Regulation Act - (i) Social purpose
(b) Bank nationalization - (ii) Twelve
(c) Banks in public sector in India as on 31 March 2025 - (iii) Regulating powers of central bank
(d) Branch expansion - (iv) More deposit mobilisation

Consider the following statements:
(a) India's Foodgrains production touched a record 315.7 million tonnes in 2021-22.
(b) India's productivity of millets excluding jowar was higher than Ethiopia.
Which of the statement/s given above is/are correct?
options: None of the above are correct.

questions.text:

A (Article No.) - B (Provision)
(a) 112 - (i) Annual Financial statement
(b) 151 - (ii) Audit Reports
(c) 267 - (iii) Contingency fund
(d) 280 - (iv) Finance Commission

(a) Difference between government's total expenditure and total receipts excluding borrowings.
(b) Difference between capital expenditure and revenue receipts.
(c) Difference between revenue expenditure and revenue receipts.

Which of the following are quantitative weapons of Monetary Policy of the RBI?
(a) Discriminatory rate of interest charged on certain types of advances.
(b) Repo rate
(c) Credit Authorisation scheme
(d) Open Market operations

Which of the following is/are the Principles of Accounting?
(a) Accrual Principle
(b) Materiality Principle
(c) Economic Entity Principle
(d) Monetary Unit Principle
When government finances its budget deficit by using printed money, it can lead to _________.

Which of the following is/are characteristics of 'Ability to pay Principle'?
(a) High income earners and wealthier individuals should pay more income tax.
(b) Low income earners and poor peoples are exempted from paying income tax.
Who is the Chairman of the 16th Finance Commission?

Consider the following statements:
(a) The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) set up a high level committee on agricultural credit in December 1997.
(b) Mr. R.V. Gupta was Chairman of a high level committee on agricultural credit.
Which of the statement/s given above is/are correct?
options: None of the above are correct.

questions.text: India's growth remain robust with _________ GDP growth in financial year 2024-25, and is projected to continue on the growth path.

Where is China constructing the world's largest hydro-power dam, which is expected to be completed by the year 2030?

Match the pairs: (Dronacharya Award, 2024)
'A' (Coach) - 'B' (Sport Discipline)
(a) Subhash Rana - (i) Shooting
(b) Deepali Deshpande - (ii) Para-shooting
(c) Sandeep Sangwan - (iii) Hockey
(d) S. Muralidharan - (iv) Badminton

Dorababu Daparti has been appointed as the Deputy Chief Executive Officer of which of the following companies in February 2025?
Who became the Brand Ambassador for Audi India in May 2025?
Air New Zealand has announced _________ as their next CEO in July 2025.

(a) 'World Music Day' celebrated annually on September 09, encourages free public musical performances worldwide.
(b) The World Music Day, 2025 has a theme "Healing through Harmony".
Who among the following has been appointed in August 2025 as executive director at the International Monetary Fund (IMF)?

'A' (Book/Poetry) - 'B' (Writer/Poet)

(b) Phir Ugna - (ii) Mandeep Aulakh
(c) Ang Asur - (iii) Advait Kottary
(d) Siddhartha - The boy who became the Buddha - (iv) Amar Kungur Boro
Which of the following won the Prestigious 'Best Magic Creator' Award at the 2025 FISM World Championship of Magic?

Select the correct statement/s from the following:
(a) 'The Youth Spiritual Summit 2025' was held in July 2025 at Varanasi.
(b) The theme of 'Youth Spiritual Summit 2025' was "Jagega Yuva, Badhega Yuva".
Who among the following has inaugurated Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT)-Power Facility in New Delhi in September 2024?

Find the correct statements about Pahalgam from the sentences given below.
(a) Pahalgam is situated in the District of Anantnag.
(b) Lidder Nallah attracts tourists in Pahalgam.
(c) It is an important transit camp on the way to Shri Mata Vaishno Devi.

Which of the following about Article 243-G in the Constitution of India is/are correct?
(a) Disqualification of membership
(b) Powers, authority and responsibilities of Panchayats

(d) Duration of Panchayats

Which of the following statement/statements is/are correct?
(a) The office of the Advocate General for the State of Maharashtra came into existence with the formation of the State itself on May 1, 1960.
(b) The Advocate General shall be appointed in accordance with the provisions of Article 165 of the Constitution of India.
(c) Birendra Saraf is the Advocate General of Maharashtra. (as of August 2025)

Which of the following is not fundamental duties?
(1) To strive towards excellence in all spheres of individual and collective activity so that the nation constantly rises to higher levels of endeavour and achievement.
(2) Who is parent or guardian to provide opportunities for education to his child or as the case may be ward between the age of six and fourteen years.
(3) Promote equal justice and free legal aid to the poor.
(4) To defend the country and render national service when called upon to do so.
Who among the following has served as the Chief Minister of Maharashtra for the longest period till September 2025?
Which of the following is mentioned in Article-349?
options: Special procedure for the implementation of certain laws relating to language.
Language to be used in the Supreme Court and High Courts and for laws, bills etc.
Facilities for education in mother tongue at the primary level.
Directions for the development of Hindi language.

questions.text:

List-I (Item in the Indian Constitution) - List-II (Country from which it is derived)

Which of the following is mentioned in Article-8 of the Indian Constitution?
options: Rights of Citizenship of certain persons who have migrated to India from Pakistan.
Rights of Citizenship of certain persons of Indian origin residing outside India.
Rights of Citizenship of certain persons migrated to Pakistan.
Parliament to regulate the right of Citizenship by Law.

questions.text:
Select the incorrect statement/s about qualification of Vice-President.
(a) is a citizen of India.
(b) has completed the age of forty five years.
(c) is qualified for election as a member of House of People (Lok Sabha).

Select the correct statements about Role of Prime Minister:
(a) Ramsay Muir - "Prime Minister is the steersman of steering wheel of the ship of the state."
(b) H.R.G. Greaves - "The Government is the master of the country and Prime Minister is the master of Government."
(c) Munro - "Prime Minister is a moon among lesser stars."

In 1950, the Constitution of India contained a four-fold classification of units of the Indian Union.
(a) Part A - Princely states with legislature
(b) Part B - Governor's provinces of British India
(c) Part C - Chief Commissioner's provinces of British India
(d) Part D - Andaman and Nicobar Islands
Select the correct pairs from the above:

Which of the following statement/statements is/are false in concern with the Governor?
(a) As per Article 158 the Governor shall hold office during the pleasure of the President.
(b) As per Article 155 the Governor of a state shall be appointed by the President by warrant under his hand and seal.
(c) As per Article 154 the executive power of the state shall be vested in the Governor and shall be exercised by him either directly or through officers subordinate to him in accordance with this Constitution.
(d) As per Article 163 there shall be a council of ministers with the Chief Minister at the head to aid and advise the Governor.

Match the following:
List-I (Subject) - List-II (Article No.)
(a) Gram Sabha - (i) 243 K
(b) Reservation of seats - (ii) 243 F
(c) Elections to the Panchayats - (iii) 243 A
(d) Disqualification for membership - (iv) 243 D

Which of the following statements are related to Article 20 of the Constitution of India?
(a) No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law.
(b) No person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once.
(c) No person accused of any offence shall be compelled to be a witness against himself.
(d) The state shall provide free and compulsory education to all children of the age of six to fourteen years.

Which of the following statement/statements is/are not included in Article 168?
(a) For every state there shall be a legislature which shall consist of the Governor.
(b) Where there are two Houses of the Legislature of a state, one shall be known as the Legislative Council and other as the Legislative Assembly.
(c) Where there is only one house, it shall be known as the Legislative Assembly.
(d) The Legislative Assembly of each state shall consist of not more than five hundred and not less than sixty members chosen by direct election from territorial constituencies in the state.
Which of the following articles and parts of the Indian Constitution enumerate fundamental duties?

State whether following statements are true or false:
(a) X-rays are electromagnetic waves.
(b) The wavelength of X-ray is very short.
(c) X-rays are having negative charge.
(d) X-rays are deflected by electric and magnetic fields.

(a) Alpha particle - (i) Curie
(b) Beta particle - (ii) Electron
(c) Unit of Radioactivity - (iii) â‚‚Heâ´-Helium atom

options: the amount of heat required to heat unit mass of the substance.
the amount of heat required to raise the temperature of a unit mass through 1Â°C.
the amount of heat required to convert unit mass of the substance from solid to liquid at its melting point without rise of temperature.

questions.text:
What were the main principles of the National Education Policy of 1968 based on the recommendations of the Kothari Commission?
(a) Compulsory and free education till the age of 14 years.
(b) Higher position and improved pay for teachers.
(c) At least 10% of gross national income spent on education.
Which of the following taxes was collected from Hindu and Muslim landlords respectively during the Sultanate rule?
questions.explanation:
Khiraj (Kharaj): This was a land tax levied primarily on agricultural produce from lands held by non-Muslims, including Hindu landlords. While its rate varied depending on the ruler, it often ranged from one-tenth to as high as half (50%) of the produce.
Zakat: This was a mandatory religious tax realized from Muslims. It was typically calculated as 2.5% of their total property and wealth and was intended for charitable and social welfare purposes, such as helping the poor and needy.

Which of the following is NOT the pattern of Urban settlement?
questions.explanation: Dispersed (or scattered) settlements are characterized by isolated buildings or small groups of houses spread across a wide area, typically found in hilly regions, dense forests, or remote agricultural lands. They are considered a primary pattern of rural settlement rather than urban, as they reflect low population density and reliance on extensive land use like farming or pasture.

In which district is the Hetwane major irrigation scheme located?
questions.explanation: The Hetwane major irrigation scheme is located in the Raigad district of Maharashtra, India. The project is situated on the Bhogeshwari river, near Pen, and is designed to provide irrigation and water supply benefits to the region. It is a significant water resource project in the Konkan region of the state.

questions.explanation: The Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act was made in 1952.
